lesson.menu_title: Follow Up or Fall Behind -- Email Chains & Escalation
lesson.menu_desc: Chase a reply, send a polite reminder, and escalate when something is overdue -- without being rude -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Follow Up or Fall Behind, Email Chains & Escalation
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Diogo Leal | Aula 20 -- Follow Up or Fall Behind

listenings.text: Hi Diogo, it is Sophia. Last class you wrote one clear executive email. But here is the truth: people are busy, and many emails get no reply. So today you learn to follow up. A follow-up is a short, polite message that chases a reply without sounding angry. Three rules make a follow-up work. First, stay warm. Start with a gentle line, like just a gentle reminder about the report. Never start with where is my answer. Second, be clear about time. Say I have not received the report yet, or the budget is still pending, or as of today, the date is not confirmed. These words show that something is open, without blame. Third, when a deadline passes and the task is overdue, you escalate. To escalate means to raise the issue to a manager. You stay polite, but you loop in your manager and you mark the email urgent, so the right people act fast. Remember: follow up, do not fall behind. A calm reminder today saves a crisis next week. You are ready. Talk soon.

Subject: Reminder, Project Atlas budget, still pending. Dear Maria, I hope you are well. Just a gentle reminder about the phase two budget. I sent the request last Monday, and I have not received your reply yet. As of today, the budget is still pending, and the deadline is this Friday. Could you please confirm it by then? If I do not hear back by Friday, I will loop in our director, David, so the launch is not delayed. The matter is now urgent, but I am sure we can solve it quickly. Thank you for your time. Best regards, Diogo Leal, IT Project Manager, Oracle.

extra_audio.text: Diogo writes one calm follow-up email. First, he opens with a gentle reminder about the budget. Then, he says he has not received a reply yet, and the budget is still pending. After that, he makes a clear, polite ask: could you please confirm it by Friday. Then, he warns gently that if there is no reply, he will loop in his director. Finally, he marks the matter urgent and closes with Best regards.
